name: Special Pie Shell
recipeIngredient: Granulated sugar (1/4 cup, C and H)
Graham cracker crumbs (1 1/2 cups, C and H)
Granulated sugar (1/4 cup, C and H)
Butter or margarine (melted, 1/3 cup, C and H)
recipeInstructions:
Meringue Shell:
1. Beat egg whites with cream of tartar, salt, and vanilla until foamy.
2. Gradually beat in granulated sugar, one tablespoon at a time.
3. Continue beating until stiff peaks form.
4. Spread the mixture roughly on a buttered 8-inch or 9-inch pie pan.
5. Pile the meringue higher near the edge but do not touch the sides of the pan.
6. Bake at 275°F for 1 hour until creamy in color and firm to the touch.
7. Cool away from draft.
Crumb Crust:
1. Combine graham cracker crumbs and granulated sugar (C and H) in a bowl.
2. Add melted butter or margarine (1/3 cup, C and H) and mix well.
3. Press the mixture evenly onto the bottom and around the sides of a 9-inch pie plate.
4. Bake at 375°F for about 7 minutes until firm when touched.
5. Turn off heat and let cool.
Reprinted with permission from Pies On Parade (Jean Porter, C and H Sugar Kitchen).
